content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 8, 1992

Judge William Moreno
955 San Vicente Dr.
Salinas, CA 93901-1513

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-353

Dear Judge Moreno:

You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-
91015.)

You have asked if, as a recently retired judge from Monterey
County Municipal Court, you must file a leaving office statement
of economic interests (Form 721) or continue filing annual
statements. You will be accepting assignments from the judicial
council that will last more than 30 days in a 12-month period.

As long as you receive assignments from the judicial council
and serve 30 days or more in a 12-month period, you will continue
filing annual statements. You will file your annual statement
with the clerk of the court to which you have been assigned at the
time the statement is due. You must continue filing annual
statements until you no longer accept assignments from the
judicial council. Once you decide not to accept any new
assignments, you must file a leaving office statement within 30
days of leaving your last assignment.

If I can be of further assistance, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question: As a recently retired judge from Monterey County Municipal Court who will be accepting assignments from the judicial council that will last more than 30 days in a 12-month period, must you file a leaving office statement of economic interests (Form 721) or continue filing annual statements?
sections.conclusion: As long as you receive assignments from the judicial council and serve 30 days or more in a 12-month period, you will continue filing annual statements. You will file your annual statement with the clerk of the court to which you have been assigned at the time the statement is due. You must continue filing annual statements until you no longer accept assignments from the judicial council. Once you decide not to accept any new assignments, you must file a leaving office statement within 30 days of leaving your last assignment.
sections.question_synthetic: A retired judge from Monterey County Municipal Court seeks clarification on whether he must file a leaving office statement (Form 721) or continue filing annual statements given that he will accept assignments from the judicial council lasting more than 30 days in a 12-month period.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The requestor must continue filing annual statements as long as he receives judicial council assignments of 30 days or more per 12-month period, filing with the assigned court's clerk. He may only file a leaving office statement once he ceases accepting new assignments, which must occur within 30 days of his last assignment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' headers but sections are clearly delineated by content structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As a recently retired judge from Monterey County Municipal Court who will be accepting assignments from the judicial council that will last more than 30 days in a 12-month period, must you file a leaving office statement of economic interests (Form 721) or continue filing annual statements?

CONCLUSION: As long as you receive assignments from the judicial council and serve 30 days or more in a 12-month period, you will continue filing annual statements. You will file your annual statement with the clerk of the court to which you have been assigned at the time the statement is due. You must continue filing annual statements until you no longer accept assignments from the judicial council. Once you decide not to accept any new assignments, you must file a leaving office statement within 30 days of leaving your last assignment.

(Government Code Sections 81000- 91015.) You have asked if, as a recently retired judge from Monterey County Municipal Court, you must file a leaving office statement of economic interests (Form 721) or continue filing annual statements. You will be accepting assignments from the judicial council that will last more than 30 days in a 12-month period. As long as you receive assignments from the judicial council and serve 30 days or more in a 12-month period, you will continue filing annual statements. You will file your annual statement with the clerk of the court to which you have been assigned at the time the statement is due. You must continue filing annual statements until you no longer accept assignments from the judicial council. embedding.summary: FPPC advises a retired Monterey County Municipal Court judge that he must continue filing annual statements of economic interests while accepting judicial council assignments of 30+ days per year, and may only file a leaving office statement once he stops accepting new assignments.
